findings.summary: generateInvoice spans 204 lines — past the 60-line threshold for a single function. Bodies this size usually mix unrelated responsibilities, and an agent editing one section often misses interactions in another.

suggested_actions.description: Extract cohesive sections into named helpers so each responsibility can be read, tested, and edited in isolation.

findings.summary: Repo ships no agent-readable instruction files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md). Agents loading this repo will not see project commands, conventions, or safety rules and are more likely to make confident-but-wrong edits.
evidence: no AGENTS.md found at repo root
no CLAUDE.md found at repo root
package.json declares bin(s): messy — agents have no way to discover commands

suggested_actions.description: Add AGENTS.md or a Claude skill so coding agents can discover repo conventions before editing.

findings.summary: Comment block appears to contain disabled code. Dead implementation snippets can mislead humans and agents into copying or reviving stale behaviour.
evidence: first code-like line: const legacyRefund = await loadRefund(user.id);

suggested_actions.description: Delete the disabled code, or replace it with a short rationale that explains the active implementation.

findings.summary: 7 direct uses of Date.now()/new Date(). Reading the system clock in domain code makes behaviour non-deterministic and couples tests to wall time.
evidence: each call observes wall time at runtime — tests cannot pin a fixed moment without monkey-patching

suggested_actions.description: Inject a clock/now() abstraction so domain code is deterministic in tests and free of hidden temporal coupling.

findings.summary: 5 direct uses of Date.now()/new Date(). Reading the system clock in domain code makes behaviour non-deterministic and couples tests to wall time.
evidence: each call observes wall time at runtime — tests cannot pin a fixed moment without monkey-patching

suggested_actions.description: Inject a clock/now() abstraction so domain code is deterministic in tests and free of hidden temporal coupling.

findings.summary: Test "renders the enterprise plan" contains no expect/assert calls. A test that only runs setup gives agents false confidence.

suggested_actions.description: Assert the observable behaviour this test is meant to protect, or delete the test if it only exercises setup.

findings.summary: Domain literal "enterprise" appears in 4 production files across 4 areas. Repeated literals often become duplicated policy.
evidence: appears in 4 production files across src, src/api, src/jobs, src/ui
representative files: src/api/plan.ts:2, src/billing.ts:7, src/jobs/plan-sync.ts:2, src/ui/pricing.ts:2

suggested_actions.description: Move the literal to a named constant, enum, schema, registry, or policy module before adding another copy.

findings.summary: calculateRefundPreview has a safe-sounding name but appears to perform side effects. Misleading names make edits riskier because callers may treat the function as pure.
evidence: name prefix suggests a pure transformation
3 side-effect signals in the function body

suggested_actions.description: Rename the function to disclose the side effect, or extract the pure calculation/read from the mutation.

findings.summary: 4 aliases from the "tenant" concept group appear across 5 files. An agent extending one vocabulary may duplicate logic that already exists under another name.
evidence: aliases found: account, organisation, team, workspace

suggested_actions.description: Pick or document the canonical term, and use aliases deliberately rather than accidentally.

findings.summary: File is 310 lines (threshold 300). Modules this large hide local coupling: small edits can collide with code an agent never loaded into context.
evidence: 5 top-level functions declared in this file

suggested_actions.description: Split along clear responsibility boundaries. Smaller modules give humans and agents a smaller surface to reason about per edit.

findings.summary: Comment appears to carry a rule that nearby code does not obviously encode. Hidden prose rules are easy for agents and new maintainers to miss.
evidence: comment says: "Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."
domain terms not found nearby: owner, plan, billing

suggested_actions.description: Move the rule into a named guard, type, config value, or test; keep comments for rationale.

findings.summary: Destination /settings/billing appears in 2 nav source files with 2 different labels. An agent updating one source will leave the others stale.

suggested_actions.description: Move destination metadata into one source of truth or document which nav file is canonical.

findings.summary: 14 TODO/FIXME markers (424.2 per 1k LOC). Each marker is intended behaviour the author flagged as not-final — too many in one file makes it hard for an agent to tell which logic is load-bearing.

suggested_actions.description: Convert each marker into a tracked issue or remove it. Lingering TODOs mislead humans and agents about which behaviour is intentional.

findings.summary: Route /settings/billing appears to be labelled in 4 competing ways across its path, file, component, page title, and nav entries. An agent editing one label may leave the others out of sync.

suggested_actions.description: Choose the canonical name for this destination and align route metadata, nav labels, and page/component names.

findings.summary: 1 local link in docs/teams.md point at file(s) that do not exist on disk. Agents reading this doc may follow stale instructions.

suggested_actions.description: Update the docs or restore the referenced file so agents do not follow stale instructions.

findings.summary: Test "creates an invoice" only uses weak assertion matchers. It may prove the code ran, not that behaviour is protected.

suggested_actions.description: Assert the observable behaviour this test is meant to protect, or delete the test if it only exercises setup.

findings.summary: describeCheckout accepts a broad `options` object with an implicit shape. Generic bags make it hard for agents to know which fields are required.
evidence: 6 distinct property reads: currency, plan, region, retry, role, status

suggested_actions.description: Replace the generic bag with a named type/object shape, or destructure only the fields this function owns.

findings.summary: resolvePaymentResult returns several object shapes without an explicit return type. Callers and agents may infer the wrong result contract.
evidence: example keys: { code, message, ok } vs { permanent, url }

suggested_actions.description: Add an explicit return type or split branch-specific results into named result variants.

findings.summary: Conditional combines 2 negative flags. Double-negative logic is easy to invert during maintenance.

suggested_actions.description: Prefer positive flag names or extract the predicate into a named helper before extending this condition.

findings.summary: 4 TODO/FIXME markers (12.9 per 1k LOC). Each marker is intended behaviour the author flagged as not-final — too many in one file makes it hard for an agent to tell which logic is load-bearing.

suggested_actions.description: Convert each marker into a tracked issue or remove it. Lingering TODOs mislead humans and agents about which behaviour is intentional.
